note: The J is the entry/all-round model of the JX family, using the same 48mm stainless-steel conical burr as the JX with an upgraded anti-static base, finer catch-cup threads and a foldable handle. It uses the classic internal-adjustment dial (30 clicks/rotation, ~25µm steps), which suits pour-over and filter brewing more than precise espresso dialing.

The J-Max is the dedicated espresso model of the J series, using a 48mm coated conical burr fixed to the central shaft for grinding stability. Its external super-fine adjustment of 8.8µm per click makes it one of the easiest hand grinders for dialing in espresso, and it ships with a magnetic catch cup.

The J-Max S is functionally identical to the J-Max in grind mechanism — same 48mm coated conical espresso burr, 8.8µm external adjustment and magnetic catch cup — with an added foldable handle and improved ergonomic knob for portability and table stability. The very fine step size makes it excellent for espresso dial-in while remaining capable across filter methods.

stepType: External adjustment with rotation indicator, stepped
note: The J-Ultra is the flagship all-rounder/espresso grinder, using a newly designed 48mm espresso-focused coated stainless-steel conical burr fixed to the shaft. Its 8µm-per-click external adjustment plus a clear rotation indicator give the most precise, easiest dial-in of the series, suited to both espresso and pour-over.

The JE is a dedicated espresso grinder using a 47mm coated conical espresso burr, prized for sweet, creamy, thick-bodied shots with prominent mouthfeel. The original JE (2018–2020) used a simpler internal dial with smaller capacity; the current JE-Plus adds a top adjustment dial (12.5µm/click), magnetic catch cup and blind-shaker-friendly design.

The JX is the original all-round/pour-over grinder of the series, with a 48mm stainless-steel conical burr (shared with the JX-Pro) that yields a balanced, slightly bright cup. Its internal-adjustment dial with 30 clicks/rotation and ~25µm steps suits filter brewing (V60, AeroPress, French press) but is too coarse-stepped for precise espresso.

The JX S shares the JX's grind mechanism exactly — same 48mm conical burr, internal adjustment, 30 clicks/rotation and ~25µm steps — adding a foldable handle for storage, better table stability and an improved knob for smaller hands. It remains a pour-over/all-round grinder; grind quality and brewing behavior are identical to the JX.

The JX-Pro is the versatile espresso-and-filter grinder, using the same 48mm stainless-steel conical burr as the JX but with a top-mounted adjustment dial offering finer 12.5µm steps and the burr/shaft fixed together for consistency. The finer adjustment makes espresso dial-in genuinely workable while the wide range covers all filter methods, making it 1Zpresso's most recommended all-rounder.

The JX-Pro S is mechanically identical to the JX-Pro — same 48mm conical burr, top adjustment, 40 clicks/rotation and 12.5µm steps — with an added foldable handle, improved grip knob and the storage/stability benefits that brings. It remains a strong espresso-plus-filter all-rounder; grind quality and dial-in behavior match the JX-Pro exactly.
A premium all-rounder built around a 48mm coated stainless-steel conical burr. The grind range runs from very fine to very coarse (~0–1400µm), covering espresso through French press and cold brew.

The K-series espresso/all-round model with a 48mm stainless-steel conical burr and a magnetic catch cup (versus the K-Pro's threaded cup). Grind range ~0–1330µm spans espresso to coarser filter and immersion brews.

The value entry point of the K-series, using the same 48mm stainless-steel conical burr set as its siblings but with a threaded catch cup rather than magnetic, and a maximum dose around 30–35g. External adjustment makes it fast and easy to dial in across espresso through filter, and the 48mm burr delivers good consistency at both fine and coarse grinds.

1Zpresso's flagship K-series all-rounder, built around a 48mm heptagonal stainless-steel conical burr with tight assembly tolerancing, a foldable handle and carrying case. The finer 20-micron stepping and 100-click rotation give more granular dial-in than the rest of the K-series, suiting it equally to espresso and pour-over.

An ultralight travel/beginner pour-over grinder (~365g) small enough to fit inside an AeroPress plunger. It uses a 38mm stainless-steel heptagonal conical burr on a dual-bearing, triaxial shaft for stability.

The current international Q2 configuration, fitted with a 38mm grade-420 stainless-steel heptagonal conical burr on a dual-bearing shaft. The heptagonal geometry grinds slightly slower than the older pentagonal burr but produces a more uniform particle distribution with fewer fines, improving cup clarity for pour-over and AeroPress.

The original Q2 burr geometry — a 38mm grade-420 stainless-steel pentagonal conical burr. The pentagonal design cuts faster than the heptagonal version but produces slightly more fines, giving a marginally less uniform grind that adds body but can reduce clarity, especially in lighter pour-overs.

A refined Q2 with the upgraded 38mm grade-420 stainless-steel heptagonal conical burr on a dual-bearing, triaxial shaft (~465g). The heptagonal burr gives more uniform grounds and fewer fines than the original pentagonal Q2 for cleaner filter and AeroPress cups.

Uses 40mm heptagonal stainless-steel conical burrs in a high-grade aluminum-alloy body (~610g). The external adjustment ring is the headline feature — far quicker and more intuitive than older internal designs — and the burrs show better alignment than 1Zpresso's Q-series, yielding a cleaner, more consistent grind.

Mechanically identical to the X-Pro — same 40mm heptagonal steel conical burrs and 60-click/12.5-micron external adjustment. The 'S' denotes the updated foldable handle, making it more compact and travel-friendly; burr material and adjustment performance are unchanged.

Uses 40mm heptagonal burrs (an upgraded X-series geometry) in an aluminum-alloy body with a removable external adjustment dial for easy zero calibration. The 12.5-micron stepped increments sit in a versatile sweet spot — fine enough for meaningful espresso adjustments yet coarse enough that pour-over settings don't require excessive clicking.

Uses the specialized 48mm hexagonal stainless-steel 'ZP6 burr' engineered for unimodal particle distribution and maximum cup clarity in pour-over — a single-purpose filter/pour-over grinder, not for espresso (range 240–1050µm). It is the grinder Carlos Medina used to win the 2023 World Brewers Cup.

Uses the same specialized 48mm hexagonal stainless-steel 'ZP6 burr' optimized for unimodal grind distribution and exceptional pour-over clarity, housed in the K-Pro body (~750g). It is the relaunched, refined version of the ZP6 — same dedicated filter/pour-over mission but with a finer, more intuitive top dial (90 clicks, shared with the K-series award-winning ring) for precise dialing of clarity and sweetness.
60mm conical-burr single-dose grinder with stepless adjustment displayed as rotations.number.tick (e.g. 1+3.2).

Commercial-style on-demand grinder using large flat burrs (Anfim uses 75mm-class flat burrs on its shop-grade models). Primarily an espresso/all-round shop grinder with stepped worm-gear collar adjustment.

Dedicated commercial espresso grinder with flat burrs, designed for a narrow espresso-focused range (170-510 microns). Stepped adjustment via numbered collar with sub-steps; the tight range reflects its single-purpose espresso role.

Compact electric home grinder with conical burrs and click-stepped adjustment from 0 to 23 clicks. Positioned as an all-round home grinder though best suited to medium and coarser filter brews; the manufacturer warns against the finest settings (0-6) which can damage the grinder.

Entry-level all-rounder using Baratza 38mm M2 conical steel burrs. 40-step external stepped adjustment ring; intended for filter/drip, pour over, French press and AeroPress rather than consistent espresso.

Espresso-capable version of the Encore using 40mm M2 conical burrs with a recalibrated gearbox so more of the 40 steps fall in the espresso range. All-rounder that can pull home espresso plus do filter brews on the same robust repairable Encore chassis.

Updated Pro version of the Encore ESP with 40mm conical burrs and a finer, higher-resolution stepped scale for more espresso precision. All-round home grinder skewing toward espresso plus filter brewing.

Commercial-grade grinder with 54mm flat steel burrs (AP = All Purpose). Dual macro/micro stepped adjustment (numbered macro ring 1-10 plus lettered micro steps A-Z) for fine control across filter and espresso.

Commercial-grade grinder essentially identical to the Forté AP but BG (Brew Grinder) ships with 54mm ceramic flat burrs tuned toward filter/brewed coffee. Same dual macro/micro stepped adjustment and digital dosing interface.
Older entry-level conical-burr home grinder with 40 external stepped settings. All-round home use leaning toward filter and drip brews; not a true espresso grinder.

Upgraded version of the Maestro with the same 40-step external stepped conical-burr adjustment and identical 200-1010 micron range. All-round home grinder for filter/drip; the Plus added a timer and higher-torque motor over the base Maestro.

Higher-precision sibling of the Maestro/Virtuoso line using 40mm conical burrs plus a micro-adjust collar, giving 40 macro steps each subdivided by lettered micro steps. Designed as an all-rounder with enough fine resolution to attempt espresso as well as filter.

Espresso-focused single-dose grinder with 40mm conical burrs in an upper-burr-rotating design that grinds straight down for very low retention and fast output. 30 outer macro steps plus a 9-position inner micro ring for fine espresso control.

Same espresso-focused Sette platform as the 270 (40mm conical burrs, low-retention straight-down grinding) with the addition of a built-in single-weight grind-by-weight scale. 30 macro plus 9 micro stepped settings.

Top Sette model with intelligent grind-by-weight (Wi) that auto-adjusts grind time based on measured output for repeatable doses. Same 40mm conical-burr low-retention straight-down design and 30 macro plus 9 micro stepped adjustment.

Budget version of the Sette platform with 40mm conical burrs and the same fast, low-retention straight-down grind path but only the 30 macro stepped settings (no fine micro-adjust ring). Espresso-capable single-doser aimed at value buyers; the coarser resolution makes dialing espresso less precise than the 270 series.
Rebadged Baratza Maestro-class conical-burr home grinder sold under the Starbucks Barista name. 40-step external stepped adjustment with a 200-1010 micron range identical to the Maestro.

Prosumer grinder with 54mm ceramic flat burrs and dual-slider macro/micro stepped adjustment (numbered macro 1-10 plus lettered micro steps). True all-rounder capable of both espresso and filter; ceramic burrs run cool and stay sharp.
Grind-by-weight (W) version of the Vario with 54mm ceramic flat burrs and the same dual-slider macro/micro stepped adjustment. Adds an integrated scale to dose by weight rather than time.

Updated grind-by-weight Vario+ generation with 54mm steel flat burrs (upgraded from ceramic), improved gearbox and a redesigned single-dose-friendly chassis. Dual-slider macro/micro stepped adjustment plus an integrated scale for dosing by weight.

Refreshed Vario (2023 generation) with 54mm steel flat burrs, a sturdier metal body, improved gearbox and lower retention than the original Vario. Dual-slider macro/micro stepped adjustment for fine control.
Mid-range home grinder using Baratza 40mm M3 conical steel burrs, a step up from the Encore with a higher-torque motor and more uniform grind. 40-step external stepped adjustment.
Upgraded Virtuoso with 40mm conical steel burrs, a digital countdown timer and a slightly wider range (200-1200 microns) than the original Virtuoso. 40-step external stepped adjustment.

Electric grinder with 29mm stainless steel flat/conical burrs and 40 click-stop settings from coarse to fine. Low-speed DC motor around 415 rpm reduces heat, with a 240g bean hopper and 120g grounds container.

Manual hand grinder using a conical burr (commonly stainless steel) with an external numbered click-adjustment dial. Compact and portable, aimed at all-round home brewing across espresso to French press.

Electric grinder with a 420 stainless steel conical burr and 51 stepped coarseness settings selected via a touch/button panel with memory function. Anti-static, direct-drop chute design reduces spill and retention.

High-end commercial/prosumer grinder with 63mm vertical flat special-steel burrs and stepless adjustment guided by a lens display indicating particle size. Powerful 660W motor at 1400 rpm, 3-8 g/s throughput, double ventilation cooling and a vibration chute giving very low retention (about 0.1-0.4g).

Electric grinder with conical stainless steel burrs and 12 stepped settings shown as dots from Fine to Coarse. Low-speed motor to limit heat, push-to-grind operation with a friction-clutch borosilicate glass catch cup that reduces static.

Manual hand grinder whose setting is expressed in three parts (rotations, number, tick) indicating a multi-rotation external stepped adjustment with fine increments. Uses a conical burr suited to a wide range from espresso to cold brew.

Electric grinder (Sage BCG600) with 40mm stainless steel conical burrs and 60 stepped grind settings via an external dial. Electronic timed dosing in 0.2s increments, hands-free portafilter cradle and direct-grind chute.

Electric grinder (Sage BCG820) with stainless steel conical burrs and 60 external stepped settings, plus a secondary adjustable upper burr for extending the range. Precision digital dosing in 0.2s increments, 450g hopper, LCD control and 50-58mm portafilter cradles.

Ultralight (about 295g) portable manual grinder (the Krinder) with Italian hardened/tempered conical steel burrs and a detachable handle. Adjustment is a coarse stepped rotary dial scaled 1-8, with large jumps between steps that make precise espresso dial-in difficult.

Electric grinder with commercial-grade conical steel burrs and 16 stepped settings (4 each across extra fine, fine, regular, coarse). Slow gear-reduction motor under 450 rpm minimizes heat, static and noise.

Updated Infinity with commercial-grade conical steel burrs and the same 16 stepped settings across extra fine to coarse. Slow sub-450 rpm gear-reduction motor reduces heat and static, with a removable upper burr for cleaning and a more refined housing.

Premium manual grinder with 39mm high-nitrogen stainless steel conical Nitro Blade burrs and an internal stepped click adjustment of roughly 30 microns per click. Excellent build with glass catch jars and very high grind uniformity across espresso to French press.

Same C40 MK4 with 39mm Nitro Blade conical burrs but fitted with the Red Clix finer-stepped axle upgrade, roughly halving each step to about 15 microns per click and doubling the number of adjustment steps. This gives far finer espresso dial-in resolution while retaining the same overall range.

Larger flagship manual grinder with a 60mm high-nitrogen stainless steel conical Nitro Blade burr (the biggest in the Comandante range) for 2-3x faster grinding. Uses the GX50 Gold Clix click system; standard configuration steps are about 21 microns per click.

C60 Baracuda with the finer-stepped Gold Clix (GX50) axle upgrade, roughly doubling the number of click stops for finer dial-in while keeping the same range. Uses the same 60mm Nitro Blade conical burr.

Rugged travel-oriented version of the C40 MK4 with the same 39mm high-nitrogen stainless steel conical Nitro Blade burrs and internal stepped click adjustment of roughly 30 microns per click. Built with a more durable polymer body and protective sleeve for portability.

Travel-focused X25 with the same 39mm Nitro Blade conical burrs fitted with the Red Clix finer-stepped axle upgrade, roughly halving each step to about 15 microns per click for precise espresso dial-in. Durable portable body with the same grind quality as the C40 MK4.

This commercial-style on-demand espresso grinder uses 58mm flat stainless steel burrs driven by a 210W direct-drive motor at around 1340 RPM. Build is heavy cast metal (about 6kg) with a portafilter push-to-grind cradle for hands-free dosing.

The K3 Touch shares the 58mm flat stainless steel burr set and 210W motor of the K3 line but swaps the push cradle for a touchscreen interface with timed/programmable dosing. Stepless micrometric grind adjustment provides very fine resolution across espresso and filter ranges.

The K3 Touch Advanced is the top K3 variant with 58mm flat stainless steel burrs, 210W direct-drive motor and an enhanced touchscreen with more precise programmable dosing and grind memory. Stepless micrometric collar allows continuous fine adjustment, well suited to dialing in espresso.

A compact Japanese electric grinder using a conical burr set with a straight-down grind path to minimize retention and keep grounds fresh. Runs a 150W motor at roughly 420 RPM with about 240g hopper capacity, weighing around 2.5kg.

A programmable automatic burr mill with flat-style burrs and an 18-position stepped grind dial spanning fine to coarse, plus a cup-count timer. Plastic-bodied consumer machine with an integrated bean hopper and grounds chamber.

The Supreme Grind Automatic Burr Mill uses flat burrs with an 18-step grind selector and a separate 4-to-18 cup dose dial. Inexpensive plastic-bodied consumer grinder with a clear bean hopper and removable grounds container.

An entry-level electric burr grinder with flat burrs and a stepped dial offering settings from fine to coarse with a cup-quantity selector. Lightweight plastic body with bean hopper and removable grounds bin.

A step up from the KG79 with a larger bean hopper and grounds capacity, using the same flat-burr design and a stepped fine-to-coarse dial plus cup-count selector. Plastic-bodied consumer machine for everyday home use.

A compact USB-rechargeable portable electric grinder using a 38mm multi-flute (10-blade) conical burr, with a roughly 50g hopper and travel carrying case. The grind dial offers fine stepless-style adjustment described as up to 80 levels from espresso to coarse.

A premium Swiss manual grinder using a 32mm hardened stainless steel (62 HRC) conical burr in a Rotating Ring Burr (RRB) system where the outer ring rotates around a stationary inner cone. Anodized aluminium body (about 705g) with a Tritan grounds cup and an Antistatic-Jig that compresses grounds to cut static and clumping.

The Trim is the slimmer etz-I variant with a 48mm midsection and lighter weight (about 665g) but the same 32mm hardened stainless steel conical RRB burr and Antistatic-Jig as the Regular. Anodized aluminium build with Tritan grounds cup and 88-click external stepped adjustment.

The etz-U is the retention-free, no-Antistatic-Jig sibling of the etz-I, aimed at users comfortable with RDT/WDT techniques. It uses the same 32mm hardened stainless steel conical RRB burr geometry and anodized aluminium construction with 88-click external stepped adjustment.

A single-dose-friendly electric grinder built around 60mm flat hardened-steel burrs in a metal body, designed primarily for espresso but capable across the full range. Uses Eureka stepless micrometric worm-gear adjustment for very fine, repeatable dialing.

The Atom 75 uses larger 75mm flat burrs in a robust metal chassis for faster, cooler grinding and excellent espresso consistency, while still covering filter grinds. Stepless micrometric adjustment gives continuous fine control with high repeatability.
A classic shop-style doser grinder using 50mm flat burrs in a tall metal-and-plastic body with a traditional dosing chamber and lever. Stepped grind collar with a numbered fine-to-coarse range.

A compact home grinder using 50mm flat hardened-steel burrs in the popular Mignon chassis, with stepless micrometric adjustment measured in rotations plus fractional numbers. Primarily an espresso grinder but covers filter and French press across its wide range.

A filter-oriented Mignon variant fitted with larger 65mm flat burrs tuned for clarity in pour over and other brewed methods, reflected in its narrower, finer maximum range. Stepless micrometric adjustment provides precise dialing across its espresso-to-filter span.

The Oro XL shares the 65mm flat-burr filter-tuned setup of the Oro in a slightly enhanced package with larger hopper and dosing accommodation. Stepless micrometric adjustment gives fine continuous control over grind size.

An espresso-focused Mignon with 50mm flat hardened-steel burrs and added sound-dampening for notably quiet operation. Stepless micrometric adjustment is read in rotations plus numbers for very fine repeatable dialing.

A specialty-oriented Mignon using 55mm flat hardened-steel burrs with stepless micrometric adjustment read in rotations plus numbers, often paired with a touch display and timed dosing. Compact, quiet metal-bodied home grinder leaning espresso but covering the full filter range.

A countertop electric grinder with professional-grade 64mm flat burrs built purely for filter/brewed coffee (pour over, drip, batch, French press) and not espresso. Uses 11 marked settings with 31 detented click steps via a front dial, with single-dose loading and a magnetic catch cup.

An electric flat-burr brew grinder using upgraded 64mm stainless steel burrs designed in-house by Fellow, extending the fine range down toward 200-300 microns versus the Gen 1. Retains the 11-setting front dial with 31 click steps, single-dose workflow, anti-static technology and auto-stop.

A 40mm conical-burr electric grinder (Fellow six-blade C6-40 Burly Burrs) aimed at all-round home brewing from espresso through filter to cold brew. Offers 41 stepped grind settings via an external collar, single-dose hopper, anti-static technology and a quiet motor.

A premium manual hand grinder fitted with large 58mm flat steel M340 burrs (the same alloy used in Fiorenzato commercial grinders), unusually big for a hand grinder. The B-Modal (multipurpose) burr set is versatile across espresso and filter, giving a balanced cup with sweetness, body and clarity.

The same 58mm flat-steel-burr manual hand grinder as the B-Modal but fitted with M-Modal (filter-specialist) burrs whose geometry produces more uniform particles for maximum separation and cup clarity. Best suited to pour over and other filter methods rather than espresso.

A versatile single-dose electric grinder (co-developed with Fiorenzato, mechanically near-identical to its counterpart) using 64mm titanium-coated flat steel burrs. A stepped, clearly-labelled adjustment collar marked in numbered dots covers espresso, moka and filter ranges, with each step clicking for easy return to a setting.

An evolution of the Allground adding a touchscreen/digital interface (the Sense control system) while keeping the same 64mm titanium-coated flat steel burrs and grind range. Uses the same numbered-dot stepped collar covering espresso through filter, with programmable timed dosing and continuous grind.

A premium manual hand grinder built around Swiss-made 47mm Etzinger hardened tool-steel conical burrs, designed to span espresso to French press. Adjustment is via a Lift, Turn and Lock crown offering 72 stepped clicks of roughly 0.02mm (20 microns) each for repeatable settings.

A heavy Japanese-made single-speed electric grinder (Mirukko line) famous for its vertical ghost-teeth conical burrs, with the burr material switched from cast iron to stainless in the mid-2010s. The numbered 1-10 dial after calibration sets filter/brew grinds; espresso is only achievable with the optional flat burr set installed.

A compact 2-in-1 grinder that works as both an electric and a manual hand grinder, using a 47/32mm conical steel burr held by a 10mm shaft on twin precision bearings. Adjustment uses a stepped collar read as rotation-and-number (rotations from zero), offering around 240 grind settings spanning Turkish and espresso through to cold brew.

An ergonomic manual hand grinder with a side-mounted crank and 40mm conical ceramic burrs, designed for filter and brewed methods rather than true espresso. Offers 8 main numbered stepped settings with half-steps in between for 15 grind sizes, selected via an external dial.

A compact manual hand grinder with conical ceramic burrs that stay sharp and transfer little heat, housed in a methacrylate-resin body with a stainless-steel shaft and handle, holding about 24g. Grind size is set by a burr-mounted adjustment nut that clicks: tighten fully, then back off counting clicks.

A slim, very portable manual grinder using conical ceramic burrs in a plastic (methacrylate-resin) body with a stainless shaft, capacity around 24g. Grind setting is adjusted via an internal stepped click nut beneath the burrs, with no printed scale.

An upgraded slim manual grinder keeping the conical ceramic burrs but adding a cast-aluminium handle, stainless-steel body and a tighter burr mount that reduces the wobble of the base Slim for noticeably better grind consistency. Capacity is about 24g and adjustment is a stepped click wheel/nut under the burrs.

A classic tabletop manual grinder with conical ceramic burrs, a glass catch jar and a hand crank, holding roughly 100g. Grind size is set steplessly via an internal nut beneath the burrs, requiring partial disassembly to change.

A revised version of the original Skerton with conical ceramic burrs, a glass jar and a roughly 100g capacity, adding a partial upper-burr stabiliser and a hexagonal hopper to curb beans flying out. Grind adjustment remains a stepless internal nut that needs the burr assembly partly dismantled to change.

The most refined Skerton, using conical ceramic burrs with a fully stabilised upper burr and spring-loaded lower burr for markedly better alignment and coarse-grind consistency. Crucially it moves to an external stepped dial beneath the burrs so grind size can be changed without disassembly, plus a strengthened crank and plastic hopper lid.

An ultra-compact manual travel grinder with conical ceramic burrs and a largely methacrylate-resin body, stainless shaft and roughly 24g capacity. Grind size is set by an unmarked bottom adjustment nut (clockwise finer, anticlockwise coarser) with no printed scale, so settings are dialled by feel and testing.

A compact electric burr grinder using a stainless-steel conical burr, with a footprint about the size of A5 paper and a hopper/catch capacity near 100g. It offers 39 stepped fineness levels covering coffee press through espresso, with an angled outlet to reduce clogging and a washable outer burr for easy cleaning.

An electric V60 grinder with a stainless-steel conical burr and a compact body, providing 39 stepped grind levels selected on a dial for brew methods from press to espresso. Capacity is around 100g with an angled, anti-clog outlet and a removable washable outer burr.

A premium unibody manual grinder machined from a single block of 6000-series aluminium, weighing about 575g with roughly 25g capacity and a 38mm Italian-made conical steel burr set. It uses a stepless adjustment knob (with a marked solid-dot reference among the dots to count rotations) for fine control, and ships with interchangeable conventional (filter-oriented) and contemporary (espresso-oriented) burrs.

A high-end, heavy (about 1.785kg) manual grinder built on a unibody machined chassis fitted with 71mm Mazzer conical burrs (as used in the Mazzer Robur), aimed primarily at espresso. It uses a stepless adjustment with 60 reference marks and a multi-gear reduction crank that makes turning easy while taking around 55 seconds for 18g.

A single-dose-oriented grinder built around a 64mm flat hardened-steel burr set in a cast-aluminium grinding chamber with cooling ribs for heat dissipation. It uses a redesigned stepless grind adjustment allowing 0.1-step micro-adjustments, with an LC display visualising the grind size for repeatable settings.

A compact portable manual hand grinder with a CNC-milled 38mm 440C stainless-steel conical burr, a machined anodised-aluminium housing and precision ball bearings, holding roughly 18g of beans. Grind size is set steplessly via a bottom adjustment nut from fine to coarse, with no marked scale.

A budget hand grinder using 40mm ceramic conical burrs with an under-the-burr stepped adjustment ring offering roughly 18 click settings. Stainless steel body with a removable hand crank; ceramic burrs resist heat and wear but offer coarse, somewhat inconsistent grind uniformity.

A compact portable electric grinder built around 38mm stainless steel conical burrs with a USB-rechargeable motor and a stepped external selector covering roughly 40 click positions. Sleek minimalist anodized aluminum body designed for travel and on-the-go single-cup brewing.

A traditional Japanese-style manual mill using ceramic conical burrs with a stepless central adjustment nut beneath the hand crank for continuous grind control. Typically a glass or metal hopper and catch jar with a classic vintage aesthetic.

A compact electric home grinder from Kalita aimed at filter and pour-over brewing, with a small number of stepped adjustment positions (around 8). Plastic-bodied entry-level design intended for convenience and ease of use rather than precision.

A classic hand-crank mill from Kalita using ceramic conical burrs with a stepless adjustment nut for continuous grind sizing across a wide range. Features a retro design with wooden or metal accents and a drawer-style grounds catcher.

A premium Japanese electric grinder using ceramic flat ghost-burrs with a low-speed motor and built-in static-reducing and fines-removing design, adjusted via a dotted scale of around 11 stepped positions. Robust build with a die-cast body, known for low heat generation and clean, uniform grinds favored for pour-over and filter coffee.

A respected Japanese electric grinder using cast-iron or steel flat-style cutter burrs with a low-speed gear motor for reduced heat and static, adjusted across roughly 6 stepped positions. Sturdy metal-bodied design prized for clean, consistent filter and pour-over grinds with minimal fines.

A minimalist hand grinder using 38mm stainless steel conical burrs housed in a clean cylindrical stainless body, with a numbered external rotation-based adjustment. Designed for portability and everyday filter, AeroPress and pour-over brewing with a focus on simple aesthetics.

A compact 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with external numbered click adjustment offering around 90 clicks for fine espresso-capable control. Aluminum body with a folding or detachable handle, positioned as the smallest, most travel-friendly model in the Kingrinder K-series.

A 48mm stainless steel heptagonal conical-burr manual grinder featuring external numbered click adjustment with roughly 90 clicks for fine, repeatable control. Anodized aluminum body with a quality bearing-supported axle for stable, low-wobble grinding.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with external numbered click dial offering about 90 clicks for finer espresso resolution. Sturdy aluminum construction with dual bearings for axle stability and consistent particle distribution.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with external numbered adjustment of around 90 clicks, offering fine espresso-oriented stepping. Solid anodized aluminum body with twin bearings for a stable grind axle and even output.
A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with a fine external numbered dial giving 80 clicks per rotation across multiple rotations (read as rotation.click, e.g. 2.42).

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with external numbered click adjustment of about 90 clicks for repeatable fine control. Robust anodized aluminum housing with dual-bearing axle support for stable, consistent grinding.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with fine external numbered adjustment at 80 clicks per rotation (read as rotation.click) for precise espresso control. High-grade aluminum body with precision dual bearings delivering excellent grind stability and uniformity.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder using an internal under-burr stepped adjustment rather than an external dial, with a wide range spanning espresso to cold brew. Aluminum body in the budget P-series, prioritizing simplicity and value over rapid external dial-in.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder with internal stepped adjustment located beneath the burrs and a broad espresso-to-coarse range. Anodized aluminum construction with a detachable handle, sitting in the value-oriented internal-adjust P-series.

A 48mm stainless steel conical-burr manual grinder featuring internal under-burr stepped adjustment and a wide range covering espresso through cold brew. Sturdy aluminum body with dual-bearing support for stable grinding, the upper model of the internal-adjust P-series.

A premium German manual grinder with 47mm Black Fusion-coated stainless steel conical burrs and a heavy stainless steel/zinc-alloy body with auto-centering burr alignment. The stepless adjustment dial offers extremely fine resolution (around 5 microns per division) and a marked zero-point for repeatable settings, making it suitable for everything from Turkish and espresso through to coarse French press and cold brew.

A lighter, more affordable version of the M47 sharing the same 47mm Black Fusion stainless steel conical burrs and stepless adjustment, but with lightweight ABS components, a silicone grip band and an aluminium catch cup (around 780g). It omits the auto-centering technology and easy-hold thumb stopper of the Classic/Simplicity to cut cost and weight.

A streamlined M47 model with the same 47mm Black Fusion stainless steel conical burrs and auto-centering system as the Classic, paired with a smooth stepless dial featuring a clearly marked zero-point for easy calibration. It weighs around 1050g with a 40g capacity and uses a magnetic catch cup and ABS thumb stopper.

The lightest and most compact M47 designed for travel, retaining the 47mm Black Fusion stainless steel conical burrs but in a packable, lightweight body. One division on its adjustment wheel changes grind size by about 10 microns, and because the system is stepless, adjustments as fine as 2 microns are possible.

An electric countertop burr grinder using stainless steel conical burrs driven by a 240W motor at around 450 RPM with a 200g bean hopper. It offers 15 stepped grind settings spanning a 280-1070 micron range, suited to drip, pour over and French press more than fine espresso.

An electric countertop grinder with commercial-grade stainless steel conical burrs and 70 stepped grind settings covering espresso to French press over a 240-1130 micron range. It features Smart Dosing Technology that auto-adjusts grind time for consistent output, plus anti-static technology to reduce mess, and a 280g bean hopper.

A compact, travel-oriented manual grinder built around 38mm titanium-nitride-coated steel conical burrs in a tough aluminium body, sized to fit inside an AeroPress for portability. Adjustment is stepless via a numbered dial lid, with settings recorded as rotation, number and tick for repeatability, and a bottle-opener pointer indicating the position.

An updated version of the original Feldgrind featuring 38mm ceramic-coated steel conical burrs and a machined aluminium unibody design for improved alignment and durability. Adjustment is stepless via a numbered dial lid, recorded as rotation and number for repeatable settings across espresso to French press.

A larger Knock manual grinder machined from a single piece of aerospace-grade aluminium for absolute burr concentricity, fitted with 47mm steel burrs that grind roughly 40% faster than the 38mm burrs in the smaller Felds and Aergrind. Its stepless, no-click dial offers very fine resolution of about 2 microns per degree of rotation across the full brewing spectrum.

The original full-size Knock manual grinder with 38mm open stainless steel conical burrs in a machined aluminium body, featuring a longer wider crank arm and larger wooden knob than the Aergrind for smoother grinding. Adjustment is stepless via a numbered dial lid, recorded as rotation and number for repeatable settings spanning espresso through coarse French press and cold brew.

An electric flat-burr grinder using stainless steel burrs, with a front dial offering 9 grind-fineness settings and a separate selector for 2-12 cups. An entry-level domestic grinder aimed at drip and filter brewing, not espresso, with a 200g bean hopper.

An electric flat stainless-steel burr grinder (Expert series) offering 17 grind settings spanning fine to coarse, plus a cup selector for 2-12 cups. It targets home users brewing drip, French press and pour over rather than true espresso.

An electric flat-burr grinder (Expert series) with stainless steel burrs and an adjustment system marketed as 45 grind settings, combining 5 coarseness levels with 9 fineness positions. Designed for home brewing across espresso-to-French-press ranges, though best suited to drip and filter methods.

An electric flat metal-burr grinder offering 45 grind settings via 5 coarseness and 9 fineness levels, with a digital display and timed dosing for 2-12 cups. A premium home grinder aimed at filter, drip and French press brewing rather than dedicated espresso.

A commercial benchmark grinder built around 98mm flat steel burrs driven by a powerful direct-drive motor, prized for exceptional unimodal grind distribution and clarity in filter coffee. The (0-16) figure refers to the newer 0-16 numbered dial scale on the adjustment collar; it is the same machine as the 1-11 version, only the dial labeling differs.

The same 98mm flat-burr EK43 commercial grinder, here referenced by the older 1-11 numbered dial scale on the adjustment collar rather than the newer 0-16 scale. It uses hardened steel flat burrs and a stepless adjustment collar for fine, continuous control, delivering the famously clean, uniform EK43 grind across espresso to French press.

The more compact, cafe-oriented variant of the EK43, retaining the same 98mm flat steel burrs and stepless adjustment collar but in a smaller footprint with a shorter, espresso-friendly chute and bag-clip holder. It excels at both single-dosing espresso and filter work with the hallmark uniform, low-fines EK43 distribution.

A heavy commercial/retail shop grinder built for grinding large volumes for whole-bean retail bagging, using large flat steel burrs and a powerful motor. Adjustment is via a collar marked in lines/numbers giving effectively stepless, fine control across a very wide range from Turkish-fine to coarse.

A traditional commercial shop/retail grinder using 120mm flat steel burrs, with a doser and a labeled adjustment collar (Turkish, Fine, Cone, Basket, French Press, Percolator) offering stepless fine adjustment. It is built for high-volume cafe and retail grinding with a large bean hopper and heavy cast-metal body, and supports SSP 120mm burr upgrades for improved grind quality.

The white-housing variant of the VTA 6S commercial shop grinder, sharing the same 120mm flat steel burrs, doser and stepless labeled adjustment collar (Turkish through Percolator). Designed for high-throughput cafe and retail use with a large hopper and durable cast-metal construction, and accepts the same SSP 120mm burr upgrades for finer grind tuning.

A compact home all-rounder grinder built around 54mm flat steel burrs, with a stepless, continuously adjustable dial (numbered roughly 1-35) that handles espresso through French press. It single-doses or uses a small hopper, has a relatively low-retention design with a knocker, and a mostly plastic-and-metal domestic build.

A 45mm self-developed 7-axis Star-Tooth stainless steel conical-style burr (37.5mm inner / 45mm outer) with a 160-spike pre-breaker core and an aggressive hatch-cut lower section resembling a ghost burr. CNC aluminum body with external numbered click adjustment (0.0167mm per click).

83mm flat steel burrs driven by a belt-driven 900W motor at ~900 RPM with a double-fan cooling system. Electronic stepper-motor grind adjustment measures true burr gap in microns with TRUE ZERO calibration for repeatability across units.

83mm special-steel flat burrs with digital micron-accurate electronic grind adjustment and TRUE ZERO cross-unit calibration. Low-RPM motor with double-fan cooling and a side-flipping digital display for chamber access.

Steel conical-burr home grinder with 39 grind settings and an integrated scale offering three dosing modes (cups, weight to the gram, and manual/time). 375g bean hopper and removable grounds container with dishwasher-safe parts.

Stainless steel flat-burr entry-level electric grinder with 17 fineness settings from espresso (1-3) through filter (4-12) to French press (13-17). 200g bean container, grinds enough for 2-14 cups per cycle, with automatic shut-off to prevent overheating.

A 48mm 7-core CNC420 stainless steel conical burr with 45-degree cutting edges and HRC55-60 hardness for fast, durable cutting. External numbered click dial moves the burr 16 microns per click across 180 total clicks.

An entry-level single-dose electric grinder with 7-core 40mm stainless steel (420) conical burrs and external adjustment (60 clicks per rotation, ~80 soft clicks) over a stepless base. 150W motor, ~40g hopper, sub-3kg footprint, includes a 58mm magnetic dosing cup.

50mm flat steel burrs with a stepless adjustment dial marked 1 (fine) to 9 (coarse), driven by a quiet direct-drive motor that grinds 60g in under 30 seconds. ECBC-certified with a recommended filter setting of 5.

Compact portable manual grinder with 38mm 420 stainless steel hexagonal conical burrs (55-58 HRC) for uniformity and reduced fines. 6063/6061 aluminum-alloy body, ~30g capacity, ~430g weight, fully disassemblable including the burrs.

Hardened alloy steel 40mm conical-burr electric grinder with 16 grind settings and a gear-reduction motor that grinds slowly for reduced noise and heat. Detachable PowderBlock grinding chamber with an embedded cleaning brush, one-touch operation with auto-stop, and a large hopper/grounds capacity.

63mm hardened steel conical burrs (Mazzer Kony type) with an infinite stepless adjustment dial and a near-zero-retention direct grind path for single dosing. Aluminum body with real oak trim, ~30g bean cup, grinds at 1-2g/sec at low RPM for low heat, with a safety-interlock lid.

A compact electric grinder with a choice of 48mm or 65mm in-house Mizen conical burrs and stepless adjustment marked for easy dialing in. CNC-machined aluminum components to sub-10-micron tolerances, a brushless DC motor with planetary gearset, and a built-in clicker giving dynamic retention under 0.1g for single dosing.

An ultra-compact single-dose electric grinder using 48mm in-house Moonshine conical burrs, with stepless adjustment via the threaded single-dose collar tensioned by three internal springs (10 large dots plus 4 minor dots between each). Aerospace-grade aluminum body throughout.

The same ultra-compact single-dose Lagom Mini platform fitted with the original 38mm Obsidian conical burrs (since discontinued in favor of Moonshine). Stepless threaded-collar adjustment, spring-tensioned, with a dotted marking scale, in an all-aluminum body.

64mm flat burrs (in-house Mizen or SSP Red Speed) with stepless adjustment at 10-micron resolution and variable RPM control (300-1400). 300W BLDC motor, CNC-machined aluminum body with permanent burr alignment and component tolerances of +/-0.02mm.

An Etzinger hardened steel conical burr set (48mm OD ring burr with 32mm cone) in a large-capacity manual grinder. Dual adjustment with stepped macro and tool-free stepless micro, NTN Japanese roller bearings, a stainless drive train, and an anodized aluminum hopper tube (up to ~50g) with silicone sleeve.

40mm stainless steel conical burrs with 15 main settings and 29 intermediate micro-adjustments for fine to coarse control. Removable upper and lower burrs and a chuteless design for easy cleaning, a one-touch timer that remembers the last setting, and a UV-tinted hopper.

65mm flat steel burrs in a fast, quiet electric grinder spinning at ~3100 RPM idle and grinding ~5-6g/sec (a 17g double in about 4 seconds). Stepped grind adjustment dial, 600g bean hopper, and a modular portafilter/dosing-cup holder.

Compact Japanese hand grinder using a 38mm conical ceramic (hardened porcelain) burr set that resists corrosion and stays cool to preserve flavor. Stepped click-style adjustment via a stepped nut under the burr, with roughly 12 usable clicks from zero.

Updated version of the Mini with a redesigned, finer-stepped adjustment offering about 24 clicks for greater precision, especially in the espresso-to-pour-over range. Retains the 38mm conical ceramic burrs and 304 stainless-steel body, holding roughly 20g of beans.

Taller sibling of the Mini sharing the same 38mm conical ceramic burr set but with a larger hopper holding around 30g of beans for two cups. Stepped click adjustment provides roughly 12 usable settings from zero.

Second-generation Tall with a finer-stepped adjustment offering about 24 clicks for more precise dialing across brew methods. Keeps the 38mm conical ceramic burrs and 304 stainless-steel construction with a ~30g capacity, plus a reinforced central spindle and improved handle for steadier grinding.

A budget electric burr grinder built around conical burrs aimed at home brewers wanting an affordable entry point. Uses a continuous/stepless-style adjustment collar for grind size with a usable range skewed toward medium and coarse brew methods rather than fine espresso.

Long-running prosumer espresso grinder using commercial-grade 50mm flat steel burrs in a heavy die-cast aluminum and steel body for durability and stability. Stepped collar adjustment provides around 55 click positions, optimized heavily toward the espresso-to-fine range.

Stepless-doser variant (SD = Senza Dosatore) of the Rocky that omits the dosing chamber for direct grinding into a portafilter or container, otherwise mechanically identical. Same 50mm flat commercial steel burrs, heavy die-cast aluminum body and powerful motor, with roughly 55 stepped click adjustments concentrated in the espresso range.

Manual hand grinder engineered chiefly for espresso, using 48mm conical steel burrs with a double-bearing-supported axle for exceptional stability and low wobble. Stepped adjustment with around 25 usable clicks; it excels at fine espresso grinds but becomes inconsistent past roughly 25 clicks at coarser settings.

A premium American-made hand grinder with a magnetically coupled, stepless micro-adjustment system allowing very fine grind tuning across the full espresso-to-cold-brew range. Uses high-grade conical steel burrs in a precision-machined aluminum body with internal bearings for excellent alignment and consistency.

Retro-styled electric burr grinder with stainless-steel conical burrs and 30 stepped grind settings spanning fine to coarse. Features a die-cast aluminum housing, a 350g bean hopper, anti-static ground container and a digital timer/dose selection for one to twelve cups.

Updated/variant model of Smeg retro coffee grinder retaining stainless-steel conical burrs and 30 stepped grind settings from fine to coarse. Built with a die-cast aluminum body, large bean hopper, anti-static grounds bin and digital dosing for one to twelve cups, with minor feature and finish updates over the CGF01.

Entry-level manual grinder with 38mm stainless steel S2C conical burrs and an external numbered click adjustment dial offering roughly 36 clicks (about 26 microns per click). Built with a textured aluminum body, double-bearing axle for stability, and a folding hand crank in some variants.

Compact travel-oriented version of the C2 featuring a folding/collapsible hand crank for portability and the same 38mm stainless steel conical burr set. Uses an external numbered click dial with roughly 36 clicks (about 26 microns per click) and a double-bearing axle for grind stability.

Larger-capacity version of the C2 with a roughly 30g bean hopper and the same 38mm stainless steel S2C conical burrs. Adjustment is via an external numbered click dial of about 36 clicks (around 26 microns per click) with a double-bearing axle for stable, consistent grinding.

Upgraded large-capacity C2 with refined 38mm stainless steel conical burrs and improved internal adjustment for tighter consistency. Uses an external numbered click dial of roughly 36 clicks (about 26 microns per click) and a stabilizing double-bearing axle.

Successor to the C2 featuring upgraded 38mm stainless steel spike-to-cut (S2C) conical burrs for faster, more uniform grinding. Uses an external numbered click adjustment dial with roughly 36 clicks (about 26 microns per click) and an improved CNC-machined double-bearing axle for stability.

Espresso-focused version of the C3 with an internal, finer-stepped adjustment read in three parts (rotation, number, tick) for precise dialing across espresso to filter. Equipped with 38mm stainless steel conical burrs optimized for finer particle distribution and a double-bearing axle for stability.

Premium espresso-oriented C3 variant with upgraded burrs and a finer internal stepped adjustment read as rotation.number.tick for highly precise espresso dialing. Features 38mm stainless steel conical burrs, reinforced double-bearing CNC axle, and a magnetic catch cup on many units.

Larger-capacity version of the C3 with a roughly 30g hopper and the same upgraded 38mm stainless steel S2C conical burrs for fast, uniform grinding. Adjustment is via an external numbered click dial of about 36 clicks (around 26 microns per click) with a double-bearing axle for stability.

Upgraded large-capacity C3 with refined 38mm stainless steel conical burrs and improved internal adjustment for tighter, more repeatable grinding. Uses an external numbered click dial of roughly 36 clicks (about 26 microns per click) and a stabilizing double-bearing CNC axle.

Premium version of the C3 with upgraded internals, a reinforced double-bearing CNC axle, and the same 38mm stainless steel S2C conical burrs for improved stability and consistency. Adjustment uses an external numbered click dial of roughly 36 clicks (about 26 microns per click).

Refined variant of the C3 featuring upgraded 38mm stainless steel S2C conical burrs with tweaked geometry for smoother, faster grinding and improved uniformity. Uses an external numbered click dial of roughly 36 clicks (about 26 microns per click) with a double-bearing axle for stability.

Premium version of the C3S with upgraded internals, reinforced double-bearing CNC axle, and refined 38mm stainless steel S2C conical burrs for enhanced stability and grind consistency. Adjustment is via an external numbered click dial of roughly 36 clicks (about 26 microns per click).

Higher-end espresso-capable manual grinder with a wide range and a finer internal stepped adjustment read in three parts (rotation, number, tick) for precise espresso-to-filter dialing. Features upgraded stainless steel conical burrs (around 38mm) with a spike-to-cut design for fast, uniform output, plus a reinforced double-bearing CNC axle.

Compact all-metal manual grinder with 42mm S2C-042-III conical burrs (SUS420 hardened stainless steel, 55-58 HRC, 5-axis CNC cut) and double-bearing axle positioning for stability. Uses internal stepped adjustment with 16 macro and 48 micro positions at 0.031mm per click, giving point-to-point dialing across espresso to French press.

Premium hand grinder with 42mm S2C strike-to-cut burrs in martensite (high-carbon) steel for high efficiency and wear resistance, set in an aluminium alloy body. Dual-dial adjustment gives 120 settings: 24 macro clicks on the first dial refined by up to 5 micro clicks on a second dial.

Flagship-tier hand grinder with 38mm conical burrs (stainless steel on the base G1, titanium-nitride-coated E&B burrs on upgraded versions) in a premium build of aluminium alloy, stainless steel axle with dual bearings, and black walnut accents. Stepped adjustment is set via a dial at the base of the center burr, clockwise for finer.

Upgraded G1 with 38mm titanium-nitride-coated E&B (espresso and brewing) conical burrs capable of efficient grinding across all brew methods. Premium build using aluminium alloy body and grinds catch, stainless steel axle/dual bearings/crank, and a black walnut pommel; weight around 568g, 25-30g capacity.

The most compact grinder in Timemore Chestnut line, with a patented foldable handle and a slim 43mm anti-slip body standing under 5 inches tall, weighing around 360g. Uses 38mm dual-purpose conical stainless steel burrs (5-axis CNC cut; titanium-coated on some variants) able to grind dense beans from espresso to French press.

Premium mid-range hand grinder with 42mm S2C spike-to-cut 890 burrs in high-carbon SUS420 stainless steel that crush then cut beans, tuned to limit fines in the 200-250 micron range for more body and sweetness. Features an external camera-lens-style adjustment dial so grind can be changed without disassembly.

Single-dose electric grinder with 64mm flat Turbo burrs optimized for filter brewing (pour over, French press, cold brew); it does not grind fine enough for true espresso. Uses a 36-position stepped dial for clear, repeatable filter settings, driven by a brushless PID-controlled motor with adjustable RPM.

All-purpose single-dose electric grinder with 64mm flat stainless steel burrs (same size class as the Mazzer Super Jolly) giving high uniformity and low heat transfer, with upgradable burr options. Driven by a sensorless brushless motor with PID control and adjustable RPM (around 800-1200), it uses a stepless dial for infinite fine dialing across espresso to filter.

Single-dose electric grinder with 78mm flat Turbo burrs aimed at filter brewing (pour over, French press, cold brew) rather than espresso. Uses a 36-position stepped dial for repeatable filter grind settings, powered by a zero-friction brushless motor with PID control and adjustable RPM.

Flagship single-dose electric grinder with 78mm flat burrs delivering high uniformity for both espresso and pour over. Driven by a zero-friction brushless motor with PID control and Hall elements, adjustable 800-1400 RPM, rated 400W; weighs about 6.5kg.

Slim-profile hand grinder using 38mm S2C spike-to-cut conical burrs (carbon steel, titanium-coated on current versions) for high grinding efficiency. Adjustment is stepped with positions marked on the adjustment plate; steps suit medium and coarse filter grinds, though fine espresso dialing can be tricky.

Portable cordless electric grinder from the Timemore x Millab line with a top-mounted motor and a 5000mAh battery good for roughly 130 pour-over or 30 espresso doses per charge. Uses newly developed S2C 890 burrs that reduce fines for a more balanced cup, with 60 precise stepped settings of 0.025mm each spanning French press to espresso.

Flagship manual grinder from the premium Millab sub-brand featuring distinctive 44mm Pineapple conical burrs in SUS420 stainless steel whose geometry slices beans efficiently for uniformity approaching a 64mm flat burr with few fines. Built from aerospace-grade aluminium and stainless steel with minimal plastic, it is large and heavy at about 1.1kg.

Single-dose electric flat-burr grinder built around 64mm burrs and based on the popular DF64 platform (made by Ningbo Frigga); the V denotes a variable/stepless worm-gear adjustment dial replacing the click stops, giving very fine micro-adjustment ideal for dialing in espresso. Ships with 64mm flat burrs and is widely upgradeable to SSP 64mm sets.

Compact single-dose electric grinder using 54mm flat burrs, positioned as a smaller, more affordable sibling to the DF64 (made by Ningbo Frigga). It offers stepless grind adjustment for precise espresso dialing and a single-dose bellows workflow with low retention.

The original DF64 is a single-dose electric flat-burr grinder with 64mm burrs (Ningbo Frigga) that popularized affordable single-dosing; Gen 1 uses a stepless worm-gear adjustment for fine espresso control. Stock 64mm flat burrs handle espresso to filter, and the platform is highly burr-upgradeable (SSP).

Revised DF64 (Ningbo Frigga) keeping the 64mm flat-burr single-dose format with stepless worm-gear adjustment, but improving on Gen 1 with better burr alignment, reduced retention, a quieter motor, and chute/declumping refinements for cleaner workflow. Aimed at home espresso and filter users wanting low-retention single dosing with fine control.

Premium DF64 variant (Ningbo Frigga) with a fully stepless variable adjustment system offering an extremely wide, finely resolved range from espresso to coarse filter; uses 64mm flat burrs in a single-dose body. The V platform emphasizes precision micro-adjustment and improved alignment over the standard DF64, with bellows-assisted low-retention dosing.

Larger single-dose electric grinder using 83mm flat burrs (Ningbo Frigga), scaling up the DF64 concept for faster grinding and a flatter particle distribution thanks to the bigger burr surface. Stepless worm-gear adjustment gives fine espresso control, with a single-dose bellows workflow and low retention.

Variable-adjustment flagship of the DF83 line (Ningbo Frigga) pairing 83mm flat burrs with a fully stepless V adjustment system for very fine, continuous control across a wide espresso-to-filter range. Improves on the standard DF83 with refined alignment and adjustment resolution, single-dose bellows workflow, and low retention.

Single-dose electric grinder (also sold as MiiCoffee D40+) built around 40mm conical burrs, a more compact, value-oriented option versus the flat-burr DF series. The adjustment dial shows more printed ticks than actual grind steps (roughly 3-4 ticks per real step, ~19 effective settings).

Updated single-dose electric grinder (also sold as MiiCoffee D40+) with 40mm conical burrs, sharing the V1 range and the same dial behavior where multiple ticks correspond to one true grind step (~19 effective settings). V2 brings minor build/workflow refinements over V1 while keeping the compact, budget-friendly single-dose conical format.
Hand/electric-convertible hybrid grinder using 38mm conical burrs, with settings read as rotation count plus a numbered position (e.g. 1+32) for very fine micro-adjustment.

Manual hand grinder using conical burrs with a clicked adjustment measured as clicks from zero, offering a broad range from espresso to very coarse cold brew. Each detent provides a discrete, repeatable step for dialing in across brew methods.

Compact electric single-dose grinder using 38mm flat burrs, with a numbered scale that is finely, effectively stepless for precise dialing across espresso to filter. Gen 1 popularized Varia affordable flat-burr single dosing in a small footprint with a relatively wide range.

Second-generation VS3 retaining 38mm flat burrs and the numbered, effectively stepless adjustment of Gen 1, with refinements to motor/workflow, reduced retention, and improved single-dose handling (bellows/anti-static). Same wide espresso-to-coarse range in a compact electric body.

Higher-end electric single-dose grinder using 64mm flat burrs, stepping up burr size from the 38mm VS3 for faster grinding and flatter particle distribution; settings use a numbered scale (up to ~24) with fine, effectively stepless micro-adjustment. Offers a very wide espresso-to-cold-brew range with low-retention single dosing and anti-static/bellows workflow.

Compact stainless-steel manual hand grinder with CNC-machined conical stainless steel burrs and six external click-stop settings adjustable without opening the body. Built mainly of stainless steel with a glass catch jar and a hopper holding roughly 15-20g of beans.

Slimmer-profile version of the Vevok Chef 06 using the same CNC-machined conical stainless steel burrs and external six-step click adjustment system. Slender stainless-steel body designed for portability and travel, with a small bean capacity and the same coarse named-method indexing.

Rugged portable hand grinder built around 38mm stainless steel conical burrs with dual bearings on a stabilized center shaft for consistent grinding. Settings are counted as clicks coarser from burr lock (around 50 dial positions), and the aircraft-grade aluminum body has an extendable handle that folds into a carabiner-style carry position.

Portable manual grinder equipped with 38mm stainless steel conical burrs and a 60-click stepped adjustment offering roughly 33 microns per click for fine control. Constructed from aluminum and stainless steel with dual bearings on the steel center shaft for smooth, wobble-free grinding, holding up to about 20g of beans.

Flagship single-dose grinder built around massive 80mm flat burrs (magnetically mounted, CORE or upgrade SSP sets) with stepless adjustment in precise ~5-micron increments. Premium machined-aluminum build with PID-controlled fan-cooled motor and variable RPM (roughly 500-1500), retention below 0.1g, and a reverse-purge function.

High-end single-dose grinder built around large 83mm conical burrs (Mazzer-sourced with TiN coating) using a three-part rotation/division/sub-division stepless scale with roughly 5-micron resolution. Premium build with variable RPM control to suit light or dark roasts and a magnet-based Magic Tumbler for even grounds distribution.

Entry-level electric home grinder using a conical burr set with 31 stepped grind levels arranged from moka pot to coarse brewing. Features a UV-protected lockable bean hopper and an anti-static stainless steel grounds container to reduce mess, with a removable burr assembly for easy cleaning.

Popular budget electric grinder using conical burrs with a dot-marked stepped dial (roughly 18 dot positions) that rotates anti-clockwise, so the printed brew-method labels do not align with the dot count. UV-protected hopper, built-in timer, and a low-speed motor that limits heat and static buildup.

Updated version of the Wilfa Svart line using conical burrs with the same dot-marked stepped dial (about 18 positions) and the same anti-clockwise rotation quirk where labels do not match the dot count. Adds an improved lower-speed motor that produces less heat and static, plus a UV-protected 250g hopper and built-in dosing timer.

Premium home grinder built around 58mm Italian-made stabilized flat steel burrs delivering strong uniformity across nearly the entire range. Offers 41 stepped settings selected by rotating the hopper, an auto-off function, low retention under 1g, and an anti-static grounds container, with optional SSP 58mm burr upgrades available.

Integrated grind-and-brew pour-over machine whose grinder uses 48mm conical steel burrs with 80 stepped settings at roughly 18.75 microns per click. Grind speed is adjustable between 60 and 120 RPM in 10 RPM increments, letting users tune particle size and fines for different roasts and methods spanning espresso to cold brew.

High-end single-dose grinder offering interchangeable 64mm flat burr sets (blind and traditional mounting options) with fully stepless micron-level adjustment. Robust ~6.5kg machined build with a 300W motor around 900 RPM, single-dose workflow, and very low retention aimed at switching coffees cleanly between doses.

Electric home grinder using a stainless steel conical burr set with 140 stepped grinding options selected via a dial wheel, spanning espresso to coarse drip. Features cup-count dosing (roughly 2-12 cups), a low-speed motor that grinds without overheating the beans, and compatibility with the Enfinigy drip coffee maker basket.
